qualifying_org_definition: State, local, religious, or community nonprofit organization; no DSHS contract or workfare MOU required for the general volunteer lane
verification_instrument: DSHS Form 01-205 (ABAWD Activity Report, rev. 03/2026) or collateral contact signed by a volunteer site coordinator containing: client name, calendar month, activities performed, hours participated, and name/signature of verifier. WAC 388-490-0005 explicitly bars DSHS from requiring a specific form.
hours_formula_detail: State policy follows the federal default of 80 hours per month (or 20 hours per week average). Workfare-lane participants use a separate allotment-over-minimum-wage formula (referral letter specifies hours), but the general volunteer lane requires 80 hours per month per Form 01-205 instructions.
accepts_outofstate_detail: WA policy defines qualifying organizations as 'State, local, religious, or community non-profit organization' but does not impose a geographic restriction requiring the nonprofit to be Washington-based. No DSHS policy page, WAC section, or Form 01-205 instruction reviewed mentions an in-state location requirement. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), absent a state restriction, any qualifying nonprofit should be accepted operationally. Tended (CA-based 501(c)(3)) would operationally qualify under the silent-acceptance reading.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide. Washington's prior 38-county + one reservation area waiver expired January 31, 2026. As of February 1, 2026, all areas of the state are subject to ABAWD enforcement with no active geographic waivers.
submission_deadline: By the 10th of the following month (per Form 01-205 instructions and DSHS policy manual). Requalification cases: by the 30th day after reapplication date.
notes:
Washington (Basic Food) has two distinct lanes: a general volunteer lane (any qualifying nonprofit, no DSHS pre-referral or MOU required) and a separate Workfare lane (contracted sites, DSHS referral required before hours count). The general volunteer lane allows self-reported hours verified by a volunteer site supervisor using Form 01-205 or equivalent signed statement; DSHS policy explicitly states it cannot require a specific form. ABAWD enforcement expanded statewide effective February 1, 2026 (previously some areas were waived).

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial challenge of Tier 1 classification. Ran four fresh searches targeting: (1) work-registration intake and employment-plan prestep requirements, (2) WA SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026/2027 volunteer track language, (3) approved-site lists or pre-registration requirements for volunteer sites, and (4) legal-aid explainers on ABAWD community service without workfare. Also fetched the DSHS public-facing ABAWD page, the manuals.dshs.wa.gov policy manual (both the consolidated export and the section-specific page at /book/export/html/10019), and the Hunger Free Washington ABAWD page. Key findings: The policy manual at manuals.dshs.wa.gov/book/export/html/10019 contains the clearest two-lane statement: 'Unpaid work includes in-kind work or unpaid work as a volunteer at a State, local, religious, or community non-profit organization' with no DSHS referral required, while Workfare explicitly states 'Clients must receive a referral from DSHS to that site before their hours will begin to count toward participation' [WAC 388-444-0040]. The manual also states 'Can't be combined with Workfare hours to meet work requirements, as Workfare is a stand-alone activity' — confirming the two lanes are mutually exclusive. Form 01-205 was revised 03/2026, confirming it is current. WAC 388-490-0005 is cited in the manual as the regulatory basis for the rule that DSHS cannot require a specific form. No approved-site list, no MOU requirement, and no DSHS referral prestep was found for the general volunteer lane. The BFET (Basic Food Employment and Training) program is entirely voluntary and does not gate ABAWD volunteer-hour counting. No county-level variation was found; Washington administers Basic Food at the state level. Remote stance remains unknown — no policy language found specifically addressing remote/virtual volunteering. Confidence raised to high: multi-source confirmation from the DSHS public page, the policy manual (two separate URLs), and explicit WAC citation all consistently confirm Tier 1 clean with no hidden prestep.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: Form 01-205 (03/2026) and DSHS policy manual both confirm 80 hours/month federal default for volunteer lane; Workfare lane uses allotment-over-minimum-wage formula but that is a distinct lane. hours_formula=federal_80. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Policy defines qualifying orgs as 'State, local, religious, or community non-profit organization' — no geographic restriction to WA-based entities found in any DSHS page, WAC, or Form 01-205. Classified as 'silent'; federal default favors acceptance. (3) Geographic waivers: Prior 38-county + one reservation waiver expired January 31, 2026; statewide enforcement since February 1, 2026; no active waivers. (4) Submission UX: Form 01-205 instructions explicitly list four mechanisms — fax (1-888-338-7410), in-person at local CSO, mail to DSHS CSD Tacoma PO Box 11699, and upload to MyWABenefits.com (online portal). Reporting cadence is monthly; deadline is the 10th of the following month per form instructions and policy manual. (5) Form fields: Form 01-205 captures recipient name, client number (case number), activity site name (org_name), signature of supervisor (org_signature + supervisor_name), activities description, hours by week (hours_by_day_or_week), total hours, and month covered (period_start_date/period_end_date). No notarization required.
